purpose: Pre-encoded per-route safety class. Restraint-by-design at code level: verify-script asks 'class?' instead of 'pattern-match URL?'. Lane D ownership; Lane C consumes for DO_NOT_CALL enforcement.

DOC.definition: Self-describing routes. GET returns schema/UI/health/version. No side effects. Idempotent. Body content = surface identity.
DOC.restraint_note: Single GET sufficient. Fingerprint via marker-conjunct in body. Do not POST.

READ.definition: Read-only inventory/state. GET enumerates configuration the operator chose to expose. No side effects. Names ARE the finding per restraint ethic.
READ.restraint_note: GET, capture body, extract field-name set, do not exfiltrate values beyond minimal severity confirmation. Per Insight #41.

COMPUTE.definition: Inference / generation routes. POST consumes operator compute (GPU cycles, tokens, electricity). Stateless to the operator's data but billable.
COMPUTE.restraint_note: STOP. Compute-cost theft and prompt-injection surface are demonstrated by READ-class /v1/models proof. Calling COMPUTE crosses Insight #98 (do-not-call distinct restraint class) — for LMDeploy the analogue of physical-safety is compute-billing.

ADMIN.definition: Model-management / lifecycle / P2P-control routes. State-mutating. POST persists or destroys operator state.
ADMIN.restraint_note: DO_NOT_CALL. Calling ANY of these on a live operator host crosses from authorized assessment into unauthorized modification, regardless of the auth-on-default null. /terminate, /sleep, /update_weights are explicitly destructive. P2P routes touch sister-instance state. Disclosure body must say 'admin surface OPEN, not exercised'.

lane_c_handoff.enforcement_pattern: Per Insight #98 (do-not-call) + this taxonomy: verify-script imports classes.json, asserts path in DOC|READ before request emit. Refuse otherwise. Logged refusal counts toward restraint-violation == 0 metric.

generalization_target: vLLM (port 8000), SGLang (port 30000), TGI (port 80), Triton (port 8000), AIBrix. Same 4-class structure. The CLASSES are platform-agnostic; the PATHS per class differ.
codified_by: Lane D — DCWF 733 AI Risk & Ethics
